title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following Chief Constable Trotter's instructions, Molly Davis plays "Three Blind Mice" softly on the piano in the drawing room. During the performance, she is gripped by intense fear, sensing a trap and fearing that Mr. Paravicini may be a threat. The man claiming to be Trotter enters the room and asserts that he has identified a liar. He accuses Molly of having first-hand knowledge of the Lorrach Farm case and reveals he knows her true identity as a former teacher at Abbyville School. Molly confesses that her sister was the teacher involved in a tragedy where a child died because a plea for help went unread while her sister was ill.

The situation turns violent when the man reveals he is not Trotter, but Jim, the brother of George, a victim of the abuse at Lorrach Farm. Acting with a childlike demeanor, Jim holds Molly at gunpoint with a revolver stolen from her husband, Jules Davis, seeking revenge against those who failed to protect him and his brother. As Jim threatens her, Major Metcalf leaps from behind a sofa and tackles him. The assailant is revealed to be Detective Inspector Taner, who had been working undercover with Metcalf to monitor the situation.

After the assailant is subdued, the group prepares to depart. Molly reconciles with Jules, and they discover they had both traveled to London separately to purchase anniversary gifts for each other. Mr. Paravicini departs to join the police, promising Molly a gift of food and nylon stockings. As Christopher Raine notes an unpleasant smell coming from the kitchen, Molly realizes her pie is ruined.
